result.stdout:
Description:

Usage:
  Microsoft.OData.Cli [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  generate  Command to generate proxy classes for OData endpoints.

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  Microsoft.OData.Cli [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  generate  Command to generate proxy classes for OData endpoints.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Command to generate proxy classes for OData endpoints.

Usage:
  Microsoft.OData.Cli generate [options]

Options:
  metadata-uri, -m <metadata-uri>                                The URI of the metadata document. The value must be set to a valid service document URI or a local file path. Optional if config file is specified. If specified, it will take precedence over the value in config file.
  config-file, -c <config-file>                                  Path to the OData Connected Service config file.
  file-name, -fn <file-name>                                     The name of the generated file. If not provided, the default name 'Reference.cs/.vb' is used.
  custom-headers, -h <custom-headers>                            Headers that will get sent along with the request when fetching the metadata document from the service. Format: Header1:HeaderValue, Header2:HeaderValue.
  proxy, -p <proxy>                                              Proxy settings. Format: domain\user:password@SERVER:PORT.
  namespace-prefix, -ns <namespace-prefix>                       The namespace of the client code generated. Example:ODataCliCodeGeneratorSample.NorthWindModel or ODataCliCodeGeneratorSample or it could be a name related to the OData endpoint.
  enable-tracking, -et                                           Enable entity and property tracking.
  upper-camel-case, -ucc                                         Disables upper camel casing.
  enable-internal, -i                                            Apply the "internal" class modifier on generated classes instead of "public" thereby making them invisible outside the assembly.
  omit-versioning-info, -vi                                      Omit runtime version and code generation timestamp from the generated files.
  multiple-files                                                 Split the generated classes into separate files instead of generating all the code in a single file.
  excluded-operation-imports, -eoi <excluded-operation-imports>  Comma-separated list of the names of operation imports to exclude from the generated code. Example: ExcludedOperationImport1,ExcludedOperationImport2.
  excluded-bound-operations, -ebo <excluded-bound-operations>    Comma-separated list of the names of bound operations to exclude from the generated code. Example: BoundOperation1,BoundOperation2.
  excluded-schema-types, -est <excluded-schema-types>            Comma-separated list of the names of entity types to exclude from the generated code. Example: EntityType1,EntityType2,EntityType3.
  ignore-unexpected-elements, -iue                               This flag indicates whether to ignore unexpected elements and attributes in the metadata document and generate the client code if any.
  outputdir, -o <outputdir> (REQUIRED)                           Full path to output directory.
  service-name, -s <service-name>                                Human-readable display name of the service instance. If not provided, the default name 'OData Service' is used.
  -?, -h, --help                                                 Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Command to generate proxy classes for OData endpoints.

Usage:
  Microsoft.OData.Cli generate [options]

Options:
  metadata-uri, -m <metadata-uri>                                The URI of the metadata document. The value must be set to a valid service document URI or a local file path. Optional if config file is specified. If specified, it will take precedence over the value in config file.
  config-file, -c <config-file>                                  Path to the OData Connected Service config file.
  file-name, -fn <file-name>                                     The name of the generated file. If not provided, the default name 'Reference.cs/.vb' is used.
  custom-headers, -h <custom-headers>                            Headers that will get sent along with the request when fetching the metadata document from the service. Format: Header1:HeaderValue, Header2:HeaderValue.
  proxy, -p <proxy>                                              Proxy settings. Format: domain\user:password@SERVER:PORT.
  namespace-prefix, -ns <namespace-prefix>                       The namespace of the client code generated. Example:ODataCliCodeGeneratorSample.NorthWindModel or ODataCliCodeGeneratorSample or it could be a name related to the OData endpoint.
  enable-tracking, -et                                           Enable entity and property tracking.
  upper-camel-case, -ucc                                         Disables upper camel casing.
  enable-internal, -i                                            Apply the "internal" class modifier on generated classes instead of "public" thereby making them invisible outside the assembly.
  omit-versioning-info, -vi                                      Omit runtime version and code generation timestamp from the generated files.
  multiple-files                                                 Split the generated classes into separate files instead of generating all the code in a single file.
  excluded-operation-imports, -eoi <excluded-operation-imports>  Comma-separated list of the names of operation imports to exclude from the generated code. Example: ExcludedOperationImport1,ExcludedOperationImport2.
  excluded-bound-operations, -ebo <excluded-bound-operations>    Comma-separated list of the names of bound operations to exclude from the generated code. Example: BoundOperation1,BoundOperation2.
  excluded-schema-types, -est <excluded-schema-types>            Comma-separated list of the names of entity types to exclude from the generated code. Example: EntityType1,EntityType2,EntityType3.
  ignore-unexpected-elements, -iue                               This flag indicates whether to ignore unexpected elements and attributes in the metadata document and generate the client code if any.
  outputdir, -o <outputdir> (REQUIRED)                           Full path to output directory.
  service-name, -s <service-name>                                Human-readable display name of the service instance. If not provided, the default name 'OData Service' is used.
  -?, -h, --help                                                 Show help and usage information
